diary: Grey Tuesday. The rain started before my coffee was even ready, and I knew it'd be one of those days—the kind where you move slowly and deliberately. The drive to Ballard was fine; everyone was patient with the weather. Spent the morning lost in mid-century periodicals, their yellowed pages still holding so much history. There's something meditative about cataloging when the light outside is soft and endless. Grabbed soup at Mighty O to warm up around noon, then pushed through the afternoon as the rain finally lifted around two. By the time I got home, the roads were already drying out. Picked up some things at Whole Foods, checked on my brewing batch in the garage—it's coming along nicely—and made a simple dinner. Spent the evening on the couch with a podcast about digitization and a book, feeling the low hum of a day well-spent but also relieved to just rest.
thought_process: The rain shaped everything today without being dramatic about it. I wasn't rushing; the weather gave me permission to slow down, and I leaned into that at work. The SR-520 closure didn't affect my route, which felt like a small grace. My mood stayed steady but quiet—not sad, just reflective. I think cataloging those old materials in grey light does that to me. Around mid-afternoon, when the rain cleared, I felt a subtle shift toward relief. I chose to keep the evening simple: just the brewing batch, a good meal, and quiet media. Eight hours of sleep felt necessary and earned. The day had a rhythm to it, nothing forced.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
